## 1. Executive Summary

[COMPANY NAME] is interested in evaluating AIOS MVP, a deterministic safety kernel for autonomous AI agents, for potential deployment in [USE CASE / SECTOR].

This Letter of Intent expresses our preliminary interest in:
- [ ] Licensing AIOS MVP for production use
- [ ] Pilot deployment (proof-of-concept)
- [ ] Integration with existing AI infrastructure
- [ ] Custom development / white-label implementation

## 7. Next Steps & Timeline

We propose the following engagement sequence:

| Milestone | Target Date | Owner |
|-----------|-------------|-------|
| Technical discovery call | [DATE] | ADRION 369 + [PROSPECT] |
| Pilot agreement finalized | [DATE] | Legal teams |
| Pilot environment setup | [DATE] | ADRION 369 |
| Pilot phase begins | [DATE] | Joint team |
| Results evaluation | [DATE] | [PROSPECT] |
| Production deployment decision | [DATE] | [PROSPECT] steering committee |

---

## 8. Evaluation Criteria

**Technical:**
- [ ] Latency meets <200ms P99 target
- [ ] Integration complexity acceptable
- [ ] Scalability proven (load test results)
- [ ] Security audit passed

**Governance:**
- [ ] Guardian Laws verified (G1-G9 compliance)
- [ ] Audit trail functional and complete
- [ ] Compliance mapping to regulatory requirements clear

**Commercial:**
- [ ] Licensing terms acceptable
- [ ] Support model meets expectations
- [ ] ROI business case validated
- [ ] Total cost of ownership reasonable

---

## 9. Legal & Confidentiality

- [ ] Both parties agree to keep information confidential (12 months post-decision)
- [ ] NDA in place (or willing to execute standard ADRION 369 NDA)
- [ ] Data processing agreement (DPA) will be negotiated if proceeding to production

---
